claim.observation: windows.netscan shows TCPv4 offset 0x7b862500: LocalAddr 10.10.4.5 port 59361 -> ForeignAddr 10.10.254.1 port 61613, State ESTABLISHED, PID 1156, Owner rubyw.exe. A second entry (0x7ccda9d0) shows the same PID 1156 rubyw.exe with a 10.10.4.5 -> 10.10.254.1:61613 connection in state CLOSED.
claim.interpretation: At acquisition time the process rubyw.exe (PID 1156) on this host held an active (ESTABLISHED) outbound TCP socket from 10.10.4.5:59361 to 10.10.254.1:61613, and had a prior session to the same host:port that is now CLOSED. Both endpoints are RFC1918 internal addresses, so this is host-to-host internal traffic (not external egress). rubyw.exe is the console-less Ruby interpreter, not a standard Windows service binary, and a Ruby runtime maintaining a live outbound connection on a non-standard high port (61613) to another internal host is anomalous and is the highest-priority triage lead for a possible interactive remote-access/pivot channel. netscan establishes ONLY that this socket existed in this state; it does not establish the peer's role, that any data was transferred, or any tool/actor attribution. Corroboration required before classifying it (process command line, the on-disk rubyw.exe image and its hash, loaded modules/handles, and the role of 10.10.254.1).
